ground_truth.title: Best Travel Insurance for Seniors in 2025
ground_truth.author: Jeff Hoyt
ground_truth.main_content: Best Travel Insurance for Seniors in 2025. We chose Allianz as the best travel insurance for seniors thanks to their high coverage limits, customizable plans, and optional coverage for preexisting conditions. Our team spent over 100 hours researching and comparing plans before choosing Allianz as our top pick for their optional preexisting condition coverage and excellent travel expense protection. We evaluated each plan using important factors like cost, customer service, plan options, coverage amounts, and more. If you plan on traveling abroad or domestically, we recommend purchasing travel insurance for some added peace of mind. Travel insurance offers protection when you find yourself in an unexpected situation, such as an injury that requires emergency medical care, lost luggage, or canceled flights. We chose Allianz, a trusted travel insurance provider for over a century, because many of their policies offer protection for those with preexisting medical conditions. Allianz offers 24/7 assistance and a variety of plans, whether you're planning a single trip or multiple trips with several destinations. One of their most popular plans is OneTrip Prime, which provides free coverage for children 17 and under when traveling with a parent or grandparent. We think that OneTrip Prime offers the best value. You get ample coverage without having to pay the higher cost for OneTrip Premier, which would equate to just over 10 percent of the cost of your trip. International Medical Group earned our pick as the best international travel insurance for their affordable rates for longer trips, along with comprehensive coverage for medical expenses and evacuation. John Hancock offers streamlined travel insurance packages with ample protection for older adults. Although on the pricier side, John Hancock has three straightforward plans ranked Gold, Silver, and Bronze, depending on how much coverage you need.
